title: Complete OSC 21 special-color storage
description: Finish Kitty OSC 21 beyond the representable first slice by adding terminal/front-end storage and rendering paths for cursor_text, selection_foreground, selection_background, visual_bell, and transparent background slots. Preserve OSC 21 query semantics, decide whether replies should be batched into one OSC 21 response, and add focused tests plus conformance evidence.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: add no new crates. Reuse existing TermColors, NamedColor indices, ColorRequest PTY reply path, renderable_content selection rendering, and frontend background plumbing. Store OSC 21 special colors in small in-house terminal state rather than adding a theme/color framework. Rejected alternatives: depending on a CSS color crate (parsing is already in-house), moving all renderer colors into backend in one refactor, or implementing transparent-background compositor behavior before the existing background pipeline is understood. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Completed the OSC 21 special-color storage/query slice and wired the special colors into existing render paths: added keyed slots for cursor_text, selection_foreground, selection_background, visual_bell, and transparent_background; selection colors now affect grid selection rendering and Ghostty shader uniforms; cursor_text now affects block-cursor glyph inversion and Ghostty shader uniforms; queries/resets use real ColorRequest/TermColors slots. Decision: keep one OSC 21 reply per requested key, matching the existing dynamic-color reply path instead of batching. Split actual visual-bell overlay and transparent-background compositor behavior into yzt-deq because those require renderer/window policy beyond safe color storage. Verified cargo fmt --check; rio-backend kitty_color tests; nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm; nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm --features wgpu; python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify; git diff --check. Only observed warning is the preexisting rio-window X11 IME function-cast warning.

title: Add OS-backed OSC 99 notification handles and activation callbacks
description: Extend Rio's notifier layer so OSC 99 notification IDs map to platform notification handles. Support OS-level update/replace and close where platforms expose it, and route activation/button/close callbacks back to the originating PTY when a=report or c=1 request reports. This is intentionally split from the backend protocol slice because rio-notifier currently accepts only title/body and discards OS notification IDs.
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision before Rust work: use existing rio-notifier dependencies only. Production crates: existing zbus on Linux, existing objc2-user-notifications on macOS, existing windows crate on Windows. Dev-only crates: none. New crates rejected: notify-rust/libnotify wrapper, because zbus is already present and direct D-Bus access is needed for notification IDs, CloseNotification, ActionInvoked, and NotificationClosed. In-house logic: OSC 99 routing metadata, notification ID/action formatting, small Linux handle registry. Packaging impact: no new dependencies.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 99 OS-backed notification routing. Backend now preserves a=report, c=1, p=buttons, route id, and close metadata; emits KittyNotification and CloseKittyNotification events; and Linux support queries advertise action/close/buttons. Frontend sends activation/button/close reports back to the originating PTY. rio-notifier now has NotificationRequest/Tracking/Event APIs, Linux D-Bus replaces_id tracking, CloseNotification support, ActionInvoked/NotificationClosed signal routing, and explicit untracked behavior for macOS/Windows until their delegate/event layers exist. Updated docs. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p rio-notifier, cargo test -p rio-backend ... kitty_notification, cargo check -p rioterm, cargo build -p rioterm, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, git diff --check, and a live Rio OSC 99 smoke.

title: Bring yazelix-terminal to Ghostty parity
description:
Make yazelix-terminal a Rio-derived terminal that can plausibly replace Ghostty for Yazelix: Ghostty-compatible cursor shader behavior, modern terminal protocol parity, Yazelix mode without terminal-native workspace ownership, and validated runtime behavior against Ghostty, WezTerm, Kitty specs, Zellij, Yazi, and Helix.

This is an experiment repo. Main Yazelix integration waits until local evidence proves the terminal is viable.

title: Record Rio fork lineage and experiment guardrails
description: Record upstream Rio base commit, remotes, license/attribution boundary, reference-source paths, branch policy, and the rule that main Yazelix integration waits for evidence. Acceptance: docs exist, repo builds or baseline build failure is recorded, and no feature work starts without lineage notes.
close_reason: Completed: recorded Rio upstream lineage, experiment remotes, license/source guardrails, reference clone commits, main Yazelix integration boundary, hard-problem rule, and baseline toolchain status.

title: Validate core protocols through Zellij Yazi and Helix
description: Validate Kitty graphics, Kitty keyboard, Sixel/iTerm2 images, OSC 8, OSC 52, bracketed paste, focus events, SGR mouse, synchronized output, and image-preview flows through the Yazelix stack.
notes:
Integration validation will avoid changing or pushing the main Yazelix repo. Use the built yazelix-terminal binary as host where possible, prefer current packaged/installed Yazelix runtime entrypoints, and separate automatic PTY/protocol fixture evidence from manual Zellij/Yazi/Helix screenshots. If a workflow requires human interaction, document exact reproduction steps and leave code untouched.

Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new production crates. Reconnect existing Sixel/iTerm atlas graphics to the renderer's current image-overlay path using in-house helper code; reuse existing GraphicDataEntry and GraphicOverlay storage rather than adding a parallel renderer. Keep terminal child identity truthful by advertising Rio capabilities through TERM/TERM_PROGRAM while exposing a Yazelix-specific host marker separately. Rejected alternatives: restoring the retired rich-text graphics pipeline, copying Kitty implementation code, adding an image/rendering crate, or forcing app-specific Yazi configuration. Packaging impact: none for this experiment repo.
close_reason: Completed: validated the Yazelix stack under target/debug/rio --yazelix with WGPU/GL. Fixed atlas Sixel/iTerm rendering by bridging atlas graphics into the renderer image-overlay path, fixed Kitty U=1 virtual-placement rendering by inferring omitted c/r dimensions from image and cell metrics, corrected virtual source rectangles to the renderer shader shape, and cleaned Yazelix host child terminal identity. Verified focused rioterm and rio-backend tests, WGPU build, conformance verify, git diff --check, direct Sixel screenshot, direct Kitty unicode-placeholder screenshot, Yazi Kitty preview through Zellij/Yazelix, and Helix-in-stack screenshot evidence.

title: Implement OSC 133 semantic prompts and command zones
description: Bring shell semantic prompt/command zone behavior to Ghostty-grade parity. Include parser behavior, rendering/selection policy, and shell integration validation.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new production crates. Build OSC 133 parsing in-house in rio-backend's existing typed OSC parser because the grammar is small and existing helpers already parse separator-split OSC payloads. Store terminal semantic state in existing grid rows and Crosswords state instead of adding a frontend protocol bus. Rejected alternatives: importing Ghostty parser code because it is Zig/C-facing and not a Rust dependency boundary; using a broad parser crate because key=value semicolon parsing is trivial; adding per-cell semantic metadata now because row-level prompt/input/output zones are enough for prompt navigation and selection boundaries and avoid expanding the hot 8-byte Square representation. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 133 semantic prompt foundation: typed parser for A/B/C/D/I/L/N/P actions and Ghostty/Kitty options, production OSC dispatch to Handler::semantic_prompt, durable row-level semantic zones for prompt, prompt continuation, input, and output, and tests for parser, real byte-stream dispatch, and Crosswords row state. Verified cargo fmt --check, rio-backend semantic_prompt tests with platform features, conformance verify, git diff --check, and rioterm cargo check with platform features. Created a follow-up for prompt navigation and command-output selection actions on top of this state.

title: Implement Kitty OSC 66 text sizing
description: Implement Kitty text sizing support if absent or incomplete. Validate against Kitty spec and Ghostty behavior without copying Kitty GPL implementation code.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new crates. Implement the OSC 66 parser in rio-backend's existing OSC helper layer and implement the first parity slice in Crosswords by honoring the declared width metadata during grid insertion. Build rendering scale metadata in-house only if it can fit existing Row/Square side-table ownership without widening Square. Rejected alternatives: copying Kitty's GPL implementation; adding HarfBuzz or osc66 as a dependency because terminal-side OSC 66 support consumes explicit client metadata and does not need to shape text itself; implementing full multi-size renderer first because width support is the protocol-detection and complex-script correctness gate. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented the OSC 66 protocol foundation and width support: typed parser for scale, declared width, fractional scale, alignment, and text payloads; production OSC dispatch to Handler::input_sized_text; Crosswords insertion that advances by client-declared width and scale for protocol detection and complex-script width control; and focused parser, byte-stream dispatch, and cursor-advance tests. Verified text_sizing and osc66 rio-backend tests with platform features, conformance verify, git diff --check, and rioterm cargo check with platform features. Created a follow-up for full visual scale/fraction rendering.

title: Harden clipboard notification and identity protocols
description: Close protocol gaps around OSC 52 clipboard policy, OSC 9/777/99 notifications, XTGETTCAP/DA/XTVERSION, terminal identity, and terminfo behavior.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new crates. Implement Kitty OSC 99 parsing in rio-backend's OSC helper layer and reuse the existing RioEvent::DesktopNotification path for display. Use existing simd_base64 and simd_utf8 helpers for encoded payloads. Build a small in-house pending notification map for chunked title/body payloads; do not copy Kitty GPL code. Rejected alternatives: adding a notification protocol crate, implementing OS notification lifecycle callbacks before parser/display support, or changing frontend notification policy before the backend supports the protocol. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented the first protocol-hardening slice: Kitty OSC 99 parser for metadata/title/body/base64 payloads, production dispatch through Handler::kitty_notification, Crosswords chunked title/body accumulation by notification id, reuse of existing DesktopNotification event, OSC 99 conformance fixture, and follow-up beads for OSC 52/identity truthfulness plus OSC 99 lifecycle replies. Verified kitty_notification tests, cargo fmt --check, conformance verify, git diff --check, and rioterm cargo check with platform features.

title: Adopt parser fuzz and robustness strategy
description: Study Ghostty/WezTerm parser robustness practices and add a practical Rust/Rio fuzz or fixture strategy for OSC/CSI/APC edge cases.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new crates for the first robustness slice. Add deterministic in-tree parser fuzz-smoke coverage using a tiny LCG and the existing parser test dispatcher so CI can run it everywhere. Document when to graduate to cargo-fuzz/libFuzzer if parser bugs appear or corpus minimization becomes important. Rejected alternatives: adding proptest/quickcheck just for byte noise, adding cargo-fuzz before there is a stable corpus boundary, or shelling out to external fuzzers in default checks. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Completed parser robustness first slice: added deterministic dependency-free parser noise smoke coverage, documented the fuzzing escalation strategy, and verified with cargo fmt, focused rio-backend parser test, conformance fixture verification, git diff --check, and rioterm cargo check.

title: Benchmark performance and graphics stability against Ghostty
description: Measure startup, frame stability, shader overhead, scrollback/render behavior, and graphics workloads against Ghostty where practical. Record repeatable local commands and evidence.
close_reason: Completed local release-build benchmark pass against Ghostty 1.3.1. Added docs/yazelix/performance_and_graphics_benchmark.md with controlled startup/exit timings, scrollback stress smoke timings, screenshots, raw CSV artifacts, methodology, and limitations. Added artifacts/benchmarks/startup_exit_2026_05_31.csv, artifacts/benchmarks/scroll_stress_2026_05_31.csv, artifacts/benchmarks/screenshots/ghostty_shader_probe.png, and the no-shader Rio WGPU config used for comparison. Linked the evidence from the Ghostty parity contract and filed yzt-7p3.39 for deeper frame-time instrumentation. Verified release build, benchmark commands, python stats readback, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, and git diff --check.

title: Evaluate frontier Kitty protocols
description: Evaluate Kitty multiple cursors, file transfer, drag/drop, arbitrary-region styling, and other frontier extensions after core protocols and shader parity are stable.
notes: Outcome documented in docs/yazelix/frontier_kitty_protocols.md. Accepted immediate Ghostty-parity protocol gaps: OSC 21 color control, OSC 5522 rich clipboard, and end-to-end OSC 22 pointer shapes. Accepted should/frontier follow-ups: Kitty multiple cursors, keyboard completeness audit, unscrolling CSI +T, DECCARA all-SGR region styling, file-transfer security-policy design, and OSC 72 drag/drop tracking. Existing beads already cover OSC 133 prompt actions, OSC 66 visual rendering, OSC 99 lifecycle, and WGPU Vulkan validation.
close_reason: Documented frontier Kitty protocol classification and created follow-up beads yzt-7p3.23 through yzt-7p3.31 for accepted protocol work.

title: Fix WGPU launch and shader screenshot validation environment
description: Resolve or bypass the local WGPU/native graphics launch blocker so Ghostty-compatible custom shaders can be visually validated with screenshots. Current failures: native Vulkan ERROR_INCOMPATIBLE_DRIVER; WGPU/GL incompatible_surface_backends; WGPU/Vulkan CreateSurfaceError; host launch outside Nix missing Wayland/X11 dynamic helper libraries. This should produce a reliable launch recipe or packaging fix, then capture the Ghostty cursor probe screenshot.
notes: Working hypothesis: prefer a reproducible launch wrapper over host-specific fixes. First try Nix-provided graphics wrappers and backend/env combinations; only mutate host packages if the missing dependency is clear and the command is non-destructive. Success requires a screenshot artifact from the Ghostty cursor shader probe.
close_reason: Resolved the shader screenshot validation blocker by passing Sugarloaf's raw display handle into wgpu::InstanceDescriptor, adding a repeatable launch-wgpu-shader-screenshot harness command, and capturing artifacts/shader_probe/screenshots/wgpu_shader_probe_gl.png. Verified WGPU/GL launch with Ghostty cursor probe, py_compile, rioterm cargo check --features wgpu, sugarloaf ghostty shader tests with Linux window features, conformance verify, and git diff --check. WGPU/Vulkan remains tracked separately as yzt-7p3.22.

title: Add OSC 133 prompt navigation and selection actions
description: Build on the row-level OSC 133 prompt/input/output metadata by adding user-visible prompt navigation and command-output selection behavior. This should cover previous/next prompt movement, command-output region selection policy, and any Yazelix shell UX that depends on semantic prompt boundaries.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: add no new crates. Reuse existing OSC 133 row metadata, grid/search primitives, selection range types, and frontend action/keybinding surfaces. Implement prompt navigation and output-range selection as small in-house helpers. Rejected alternatives: adding a shell-integration library, introducing a generalized semantic-region index before profiling, or depending on Ghostty internals. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 133 prompt navigation and command-output selection actions. Added backend navigation/selection regressions, config action parsing coverage, and docs update. Verified cargo fmt --check; rio-backend semantic_ tests; rioterm semantic_prompt_actions_parse_from_config_strings; python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify; git diff --check; nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm. Only observed warning is the preexisting rio-window X11 IME function-cast warning.

title: Render OSC 66 scale and fractional text visually
description: Build on the OSC 66 parser and width-aware grid insertion by carrying scale/fraction/alignment metadata into the renderer and drawing larger/smaller text spans. Keep the packed Square representation small; use side-table metadata only if the renderer can consume it without broad grid churn.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 66 visual rendering slice. Text sizing metadata now lives in the Extras side table, anchor cells carry TEXT_SIZING flags, and the grid emitter renders scaled/fractional text runs from that payload without growing Square. Added backend metadata regression coverage, updated parity docs, and created yzt-7p3.37 for the remaining full multicell editing semantics. Verified with cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, conformance verify, focused rio-backend text_sizing tests, rioterm cargo check, cargo build -p rioterm, and a local CPU-renderer OSC 66 screenshot probe.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Rust dependency gate: use in-house/std only. Existing OSC 66 parsing and rendering data paths are enough for this slice; no production or dev-only crates are needed. Rejected adding shaping/layout crates because the terminal already owns glyph layout, and the bead is about carrying parsed metadata into the existing renderer. Packaging impact: none.

title: Define Ghostty parity contract
description: Define must/should/frontier parity for Ghostty behavior. Include cursor shader semantics, protocols, terminal identity, shell integration, security/source-absorption policy, validation evidence, and pivot criteria if Rio cannot support the target cleanly.
close_reason: Completed Ghostty parity contract covering cursor shader semantics, protocol tiers, Yazelix mode boundaries, validation evidence, absorption rules, and pivot criteria.

title: Audit OSC 52 policy and terminal identity truthfulness
description: Review OSC 52 clipboard read/write policy, DA/XTVERSION terminal identity, XTGETTCAP capability claims, and packaged terminfo for truthfulness against the protocols implemented in yazelix-terminal. Add policy gates or corrections where Rio currently advertises or allows behavior too broadly.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new crates. Harden OSC 52 using existing simd_base64/simd_utf8 helpers and existing RioEvent clipboard events. Add explicit size and decode policy in Crosswords rather than introducing a frontend clipboard policy framework. Audit identity via existing XTGETTCAP table and TERM_PROGRAM/Yazelix-mode environment. Rejected alternatives: adding a prompt/permission UI before protocol state is correct, changing TERM away from Rio's terminfo names before packaged terminfo is audited, or advertising unverified capabilities. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Audited and hardened the non-interactive OSC 52/identity slice: OSC 52 stores now reject unsupported clipboard designators, invalid base64, non-UTF-8 payloads, and oversized encoded/decoded payloads with visible warnings; added backend regression coverage; documented current TERM/TERM_PROGRAM/XTVERSION/terminfo truthfulness and remaining policy gaps. Verified osc52 backend test, cargo fmt, conformance verify, git diff --check, and rioterm cargo check with platform features.

title: Complete OSC 99 lifecycle replies and activation behavior
description: Extend the initial OSC 99 display/chunking support with alive/query replies, close/update lifecycle behavior where the frontend can observe OS notification state, and optional activation/focus reply handling if supported by Rio's notifier backend.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: add no new crates. Use the existing OSC 99 parser, Crosswords pending notification state, RioEvent PTY write route, and desktop notification event path. Implement alive/query replies and close/update bookkeeping in-house against the Kitty desktop notification spec. Rejected alternatives: depending on desktop notification libraries for lifecycle feedback before Rio's notifier backend exposes IDs, adding a permission UI for notifications, or broadening this bead to full button/icon activation. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented the deterministic OSC 99 lifecycle protocol slice: sanitized notification IDs before replies, parsed c=1 close-report metadata, tracked live notification IDs for p=alive replies, answered p=? support queries, honored p=close in backend state, and emitted p=close;untracked when close reports are requested but Rio cannot observe OS close events. Created follow-up yzt-0o4 for OS-backed notification handles, update/replace, activation, button, and true close callbacks. Verified cargo fmt --check; rio-backend kitty_notification tests; python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify; git diff --check; nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm. Only observed warning is the preexisting rio-window X11 IME function-cast warning.

title: Fix WGPU Vulkan surface creation on COSMIC NVIDIA
description: WGPU shader validation now works through WGPU_BACKEND=gl, but WGPU_BACKEND=vulkan still fails on this COSMIC Wayland/NVIDIA host with CreateSurfaceError { inner: Hal(FailedToCreateSurfaceForAnyBackend({})) }. Investigate whether this is a wgpu/raw-window-handle integration issue, a Nix/NVIDIA wrapper issue, or a compositor/backend limitation. Keep WGPU/GL as the validation recipe until this is fixed.
close_reason: Current tree no longer reproduces the COSMIC Wayland/NVIDIA WGPU/Vulkan CreateSurfaceError. Verified the exact shader screenshot harness with its new default WGPU_BACKEND=vulkan, captured visual evidence at artifacts/shader_probe/screenshots/wgpu_shader_probe_vulkan.png, kept GL as explicit fallback, and updated docs. Also verified python3 -m py_compile tools/yazelix_conformance.py, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, and git diff --check.

title: Implement Kitty OSC 21 color control
description: Implement Kitty OSC 21 keyed color control for Yazelix-terminal. Scope: parse set/query/reset packets; support foreground, background, cursor, cursor_text, selection foreground/background, visual_bell where representable, transparent_background_color slots where practical, and ANSI palette entries; reply with key=? for unknown query fields; preserve existing OSC 10/11/12 and palette reset behavior; add conformance fixtures and focused tests. Reference Kitty official docs and Ghostty's MIT OSC 21 parser/behavior for parity, without copying Kitty implementation code.
notes: Implemented no-new-crate first slice: OSC 21 parser, set/query/reset dispatch, foreground/background/cursor/palette mapping, rgbi plus small named color parsing for Ghostty's aliceblue fixture, unsupported query reply as key=?, conformance fixture, and focused tests. Harder remaining gap: cursor_text, selection colors, visual_bell, and transparent background need terminal/front-end storage and possibly batched OSC21 replies; filed yzt-03p for that continuation.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 21 first slice: typed parser, set/query/reset dispatch, foreground/background/cursor/palette support, unsupported query reply, conformance fixture, docs, and focused tests. Filed yzt-03p for special-color storage and batched query continuation.

title: Implement Kitty OSC 5522 rich clipboard
description: Implement a safe first slice of Kitty OSC 5522 rich clipboard support. Scope: parse metadata/payload packets; support type=read, type=write, type=wdata, and type=walias for text/plain first; route reads/writes through an explicit visible policy like the hardened OSC 52 path; fail closed on unsupported MIME, invalid base64, oversized chunks, malformed sessions, and missing permissions; add parser, policy, and conformance tests. Use Kitty docs as the protocol source and Ghostty's MIT parser as a behavior reference.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 5522 text/plain read/write/wdata/walias first slice with parser, policy events, conformance fixture, tests, and docs. Deferred arbitrary rich MIME and password trust prompts as future work.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Crate-vs-in-house decision: use in-house/std plus existing base64/UTF-8 helpers. The protocol grammar is a small key-value metadata parser and payload gate that should live near existing OSC parsing; adding a URL/querystring parser or MIME crate would not buy enough and could weaken fail-closed handling. Reuse existing base64, simd_utf8, ClipboardType, RioEvent ClipboardLoad/Store/PtyWrite paths. Packaging impact: none.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Implemented safe text/plain first slice for OSC 5522: metadata/payload parser, type=read/write/wdata/walias, MIME-list reads, text/plain read/write replies, frontend clipboard policy events, fail-closed errors for unsupported MIME/invalid base64/oversized chunks/missing sessions. Arbitrary MIME, OS rich clipboard APIs, and password trust prompts remain deferred.

title: Wire Kitty OSC 22 pointer shapes end to end
description: Finish Kitty OSC 22 pointer shape support. Current code parses simple shape names but does not wire terminal state through the live frontend cursor decision. Scope: implement normal/alternate-screen pointer shape stacks; support set/reset/push/pop and current/support queries; map Kitty/CSS shape names to winit cursor icons; allow URL hover, selection drag, and resize interactions to override app-requested shapes only while active; add parser and frontend behavior tests.
notes: Implemented no-new-crate OSC 22 support from the official Kitty pointer-shapes spec: set/reset, push/pop stack, current/default/grabbed/support queries, 16-entry stacks swapped with alternate screen and cleared on reset, frontend preferred cursor selection with URL/selection/resize overrides, and conformance fixture. Verified with focused rio-backend mouse_cursor tests, rioterm cargo check, conformance verify, fmt, and diff check.
close_reason: Implemented Kitty OSC 22 set/reset, push/pop, current/support queries, alt-screen stacks, reset clearing, frontend cursor selection, conformance fixture, docs, and focused tests.

title: Implement Kitty multiple cursors
description: Implement Kitty's multiple cursors protocol as a frontier cursor-shader feature. Scope: parse CSI > ... q extra-cursor commands; track screen-local ephemeral extra cursor state; clear on ED 2/3/22, reset, and normal/alternate screen switches; render through the same cursor renderer path where possible, including Ghostty cursor shader inputs; implement support/state/color queries; add black-box fixtures. This is Kitty-frontier parity, not strict Ghostty parity, because the checked Ghostty source does not appear to implement it yet.
close_reason: Implemented parser dispatch for CSI > ... SP q, support/state/color queries, point/rectangle/current-coordinate mutation, ED 2/3/22 reset and alt-screen clearing, conformance fixture, backend tests, and frontend sprite rendering. Exact multi-cursor shader/reverse-video parity tracked separately in yzt-7p3.26.1.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Crate-vs-in-house decision: use in-house/std only. Multiple cursors are a small CSI grammar plus terminal/grid/render state already owned by Rio; serde/parser crates would not help with cell-coordinate semantics or renderer plumbing. Rejected alternatives: generic ANSI crates and color crates, because the implementation should reuse existing Params, ColorRgb, cursor, style, and renderer data paths. Packaging impact: none unless later renderer work needs new shader assets, which should remain checked-in local assets.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Implemented Kitty multiple-cursor protocol parsing/state/queries/clearing and atlas-slot sprite rendering. Split exact multi-cursor reverse-video text inversion and multi-cursor Ghostty shader uniforms into child bead yzt-7p3.26.1 because current GridUniforms/GhosttyShaderFrameState only model one cursor position.

title: Complete Kitty multiple-cursor shader and reverse-video rendering
description: Extend the renderer beyond sprite-only extra cursors so Kitty multiple cursors get exact block-cursor reverse-video text handling and Ghostty cursor shader effects for every extra cursor, not just the main cursor. Current yzt-7p3.26 implements parser/state/queries/clearing and atlas sprite rendering, but GridUniforms and GhosttyShaderFrameState still model one cursor position. Acceptance: add multi-cursor-capable uniforms or an equivalent render path for block cursor fg/bg inversion; expose all visible extra cursor rectangles to Ghostty-compatible cursor shader inputs or document a deliberate shader ABI extension; keep single-cursor behavior unchanged; add focused renderer tests or visual probes.
close_reason: Implemented the multi-cursor renderer/shader slice. GridUniforms now carries up to 256 extra block cursor cells with independent background and text-swap colors, and the CPU/WGPU/Metal/Vulkan shader paths consult that array after the main cursor. Ghostty shader uniforms keep existing Ghostty offsets and add a Yazelix extension for extra cursor count, rects, colors, and styles. Updated docs to describe the additive shader ABI and bounded 256-cell limit. Verified cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, conformance verify, sugarloaf ghostty_ tests, rioterm cargo check, cargo build -p rioterm, and a local CPU-rendered Kitty multiple-cursor screenshot probe.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Rust dependency gate: use in-house/std only. The work is renderer state plumbing over existing cursor, grid, shader uniform, and sprite paths. Production crates: none. Dev-only crates: none. Rejected adding shader/uniform helper crates because the existing Sugarloaf grid and Ghostty shader structs own the ABI. Packaging impact: none.

title: Audit Kitty keyboard protocol completeness
description: Run a spec-level audit of Yazelix-terminal's existing Kitty keyboard protocol implementation. Scope: compare mode stack, mode query replies, reset behavior, associated text, alternate keys, event types, report-all-keys, numpad, function/media keys, and modifier encoding against the current official Kitty spec; add fixtures for gaps that affect Helix, Yazi, Nushell, and Zellij; document any winit-limited behavior explicitly.
close_reason: Completed spec-level Kitty keyboard audit in docs/yazelix/kitty_keyboard_audit.md, added all-flags and stack fixture streams to conformance/fixtures/manifest.json, updated protocol docs, and created follow-up beads yzt-7p3.32 through yzt-7p3.36 for concrete gaps. Verified with python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify and git diff --check.

title: Implement Kitty unscrolling CSI plus T
description: Implement Kitty's unscrolling extension, CSI <n> + T. Scope: parse the '+' intermediate/final form; pull lines from scrollback into the top of the screen when available; use blank lines on alternate screen or empty scrollback; respect implementation limits; add focused scrollback regression tests. This is a small modern shell UX feature, useful after core Ghostty parity work.
close_reason: Implemented Kitty unscroll CSI n + T. Parser dispatch now routes CSI + T to Handler::unscroll; primary-screen full-screen regions pull lines from scrollback and shrink consumed history, while alt screen, empty scrollback, and partial regions fall back to blank SD behavior. Added grid and parser dispatch regression tests plus conformance fixture kitty_unscroll_three_lines. Verified with cargo fmt --check, nix develop -c cargo test -p rio-backend --features 'rio-window/x11 rio-window/wayland rio-window/wayland-dlopen' unscroll -- --nocapture, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm, and git diff --check.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Crate-vs-in-house decision: use in-house/std only. This is a small CSI parser and grid/scrollback operation inside existing Rio backend structures; no production or dev crate is needed. Rejected alternatives: terminal protocol crates would not own Rio's screen/scrollback semantics, and adding a parser crate would increase packaging surface without reducing risk. Packaging impact: none.

title: Implement Kitty DECCARA all-SGR region styling
description: Implement Kitty's extension to DECCARA for applying all SGR attributes to arbitrary screen rectangles. Scope: parse DECCARA region/style sequences; apply SGR attributes to clipped rectangular regions without moving the cursor; update damage tracking; preserve existing erase/BCE behavior; add tests for clipping, defaults, and representative foreground/background/style changes.
notes: Dependency decision: use in-house/std only. DECCARA is existing CSI parsing plus StyleSet mutation over a clipped rectangle; no new parser or style crate is justified. Production crates: none. Dev-only crates: none. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented Kitty DECCARA all-SGR rectangular styling with parser dispatch, grid style application, conformance fixture, docs, and focused tests. Verified: cargo fmt --check; rio-backend deccara tests; conformance verify; git diff --check; cargo check -p rioterm; cargo build -p rioterm.

title: Build source absorption workflow for Ghostty WezTerm and Kitty
description: Create a reusable dossier template for studying Ghostty, WezTerm, and Kitty behavior without blind copying or license drift. Ghostty/WezTerm may be studied as permissive sources with attribution; Kitty implementation remains specs/black-box unless an explicit GPL decision is made.
close_reason: Completed source absorption workflow, dossier template, and dossier directory guardrails for Ghostty/WezTerm/Kitty parity work.

title: Design Kitty file transfer security policy
description: Design the product/security policy before implementing Kitty file transfer over the TTY. Scope: session approval UI, destination defaults, send/receive semantics, password bypass policy, path validation, symlink/hardlink handling, directory traversal, size limits, cancel behavior, quiet responses, logging/audit, and how this interacts with SSH/nested terminal use. Do not implement parser/runtime file writes until this policy is settled.
close_reason: Documented Kitty file transfer security policy in docs/yazelix/kitty_file_transfer_policy.md and split implementation into parser-deny, approved incoming writes, safe local reads, and optional advanced feature beads. Verified with git diff --check.

title: Implement Kitty file transfer parser with deny-by-default replies
description: Implement the OSC 5113 parser/session skeleton from docs/yazelix/kitty_file_transfer_policy.md. Scope: parse key/value command packets, maintain minimal session state, reply deterministically with EPERM for send/receive starts, reject malformed or out-of-order commands without opening paths, and add focused parser/conformance tests.
notes: Dependency decision: use in-house/std only for the deny-by-default parser skeleton. OSC 5113 is ASCII key/value parsing plus response formatting and session bookkeeping; no new parser crate or filesystem crate is justified yet. Production crates: none. Dev-only crates: none. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 5113 parser/session skeleton with deny-by-default EPERM replies for send/receive starts, deterministic rejection for out-of-order file/data commands, no filesystem access, conformance fixture, docs, and tests. Verified: cargo fmt --check; rio-backend file_transfer tests; conformance verify; git diff --check; cargo check -p rioterm; cargo build -p rioterm.

title: Add Kitty file transfer approval UI and safe incoming writes
description: Implement approved remote-to-local writes for OSC 5113 send sessions after the deny-by-default parser skeleton. Scope: user approval prompt, explicit destination root, per-session staging directory, regular files/directories only, no overwrites, path escape protection, size limits, cancel cleanup, and tests.
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision before Rust work: use in-house/std plus existing rio-notifier only. Production crates: none beyond existing zbus/notification path already in workspace. Dev-only crates: none. Rejected crates: tempfile/directories/native file chooser, because this slice can use std fs/env and the existing notification action UI to keep packaging unchanged. In-house logic: OSC 5113 metadata parsing, path normalization, staging/commit state machine, size limits, and route-specific approval decision plumbing. Packaging impact: no new dependencies.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 5113 approval-routed remote-to-local send writes: parser metadata, frontend notification approval, pending/approved session state, staging and commit flow, safe path normalization, regular file/directory writes, size limits, cancel/finish cleanup, docs, and focused tests. Verified cargo fmt --check, rio-backend osc5113/kitty_file_transfer tests, cargo check -p rioterm, cargo build -p rioterm, conformance verify, and git diff --check.

title: Add safe Kitty file transfer local-file reads
description: Implement approved local-to-remote reads for OSC 5113 receive sessions. Scope: explicit approval preview, bounded directory traversal, regular files/directories only, no symlink following, one-file-at-a-time streaming, cancel behavior, and tests for denial and traversal limits.
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision before Rust work: use in-house/std only. Production crates: no new crates beyond existing base64 already used in rio-backend. Dev-only crates: none. Rejected crates: walkdir/tempfile/directories because bounded traversal, temp paths, and filesystem metadata can be handled with std for this slice. In-house logic: receive-session path collection, approval preview text, symlink-safe bounded traversal, file metadata/data reply generation, one-file-at-a-time chunking, and cancel/finish session handling. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented approved OSC 5113 receive/read sessions: path collection before approval, notification preview body, bounded path counts and traversal depth, symlink-parent rejection, regular file/directory metadata listing, one-file-at-a-time regular file streaming, cancel/finish handling, docs, and focused tests. Verified cargo fmt --check, rio-backend osc5113/kitty_file_transfer tests, cargo check -p rioterm, cargo build -p rioterm, conformance verify, and git diff --check.

title: Add optional Kitty file transfer compression and trusted bypass
description: Implement advanced OSC 5113 file-transfer features only after the safe send/receive core exists. Scope: zlib compression, scoped trust/bypass config if still desired, symlink support constrained to approved roots, rsync/delta behavior if practical, and audit documentation.

title: Track Kitty OSC 72 drag and drop stabilization
description: Track Kitty OSC 72 drag/drop protocol maturity before implementation. Kitty marks the protocol as still under development, and full support requires OS drag/drop event integration, MIME negotiation, chunking, remote-machine behavior, and same-window security rules. Outcome: re-check the spec and app adoption later; optionally implement support-query behavior first if real applications begin depending on it.
close_reason: Documented OSC 72 tracking policy in docs/yazelix/kitty_dnd_tracking_policy.md. Decision: do not advertise support or answer support queries until Kitty's protocol stabilizes and frontend OS drag/drop routing/security policy exists. Verified with git diff --check.

title: Fix Kitty keyboard stack edge semantics
description: Audit yzt-7p3.27 found that keyboard-mode pop from stack index zero wraps to the last slot, while the Kitty spec says a pop that empties the stack resets all flags. Also validate full-stack push behavior against the spec requirement to evict the oldest entry. Scope: replace the wraparound underflow test with spec behavior, add overflow/order coverage, preserve separate main/alternate stacks. Dependency decision: in-house/std only; this is existing state-machine logic and needs no crate.
close_reason: Implemented spec-shaped keyboard mode stack semantics: compact stack, oldest-entry eviction on full push, pop-to-empty reset, and shared mode sync helper. Replaced wraparound underflow test with spec regression and added overflow/order assertions. Verified with cargo fmt --check, nix develop -c cargo test -p rio-backend --features 'rio-window/x11 rio-window/wayland rio-window/wayland-dlopen' keyboard_mode -- --nocapture, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, git diff --check, and nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm. Only observed warning is the preexisting X11 IME function-cast warning.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Crate-vs-in-house decision before Rust work: use in-house/std only. This is existing keyboard-mode stack state in rio-backend; adding a crate would not reduce risk or complexity. Production crates: none. Dev-only crates: none. Rejected alternatives: external stack/ring-buffer crate, because the contract is tiny and spec-specific. Packaging impact: none.

title: Complete Kitty keyboard modifier-bit reporting
description: Audit yzt-7p3.27 found that SequenceModifiers reports shift, alt, control, and super only. Kitty also defines hyper, meta, caps_lock, and num_lock bits. Scope: report Hyper/Meta for their own key events where Rio exposes NamedKey::Hyper/Meta, investigate lock-state availability for CapsLock/NumLock, and document platform limits if lock state is not exposed. Dependency decision: in-house/std only; this is a local bitfield and platform-event mapping change.
close_reason: Completed modifier-bit slice: added Hyper and Meta SequenceModifiers bits, applied those bits for Hyper/Meta key events, kept CapsLock/NumLock bits explicit but unfabricated without platform lock state, and documented the remaining platform limitation. Verified with cargo fmt --check, nix develop -c cargo test -p rioterm --features 'rio-window/x11 rio-window/wayland rio-window/wayland-dlopen' kitty_ -- --nocapture, nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, and git diff --check. Only observed warning is the preexisting X11 IME function-cast warning.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Crate-vs-in-house decision before Rust work: use in-house/std only. This is a local bitflag and key-event mapping update in the existing keyboard encoder. Production crates: none. Dev-only crates: none. Rejected alternatives: dependency for keyboard state, because the available state is already determined by rio-window APIs. Packaging impact: none.

title: Implement Kitty base-layout alternate keys
description: Audit yzt-7p3.27 found that REPORT_ALTERNATE_KEYS emits shifted alternates but not Kitty's base-layout alternate key. Scope: derive base-layout characters from KeyEvent.physical_key for PC-101 letter, digit, and punctuation keys; emit unicode:shifted:base or unicode::base as required; add fixtures for non-Latin shortcut matching where possible. Dependency decision: in-house/std only; the source data is already in rio-window KeyCode/PhysicalKey.
close_reason: Implemented Kitty base-layout alternate key reporting for PC-101 physical letters, digits, and punctuation via KeyEvent.physical_key. Added formatter coverage for unicode::base and unicode:shifted:base payloads and documented remaining limits for non-PC-101 keys. Verified with cargo fmt --check, nix develop -c cargo test -p rioterm --features 'rio-window/x11 rio-window/wayland rio-window/wayland-dlopen' kitty_ -- --nocapture, nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, and git diff --check. Only observed warning is the preexisting X11 IME function-cast warning.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Crate-vs-in-house decision before Rust work: use in-house/std only. Physical-key to PC-101 base layout mapping is a small deterministic table over rio_window::keyboard::KeyCode. Production crates: none. Dev-only crates: none. Rejected alternatives: layout libraries, because Kitty asks for standard PC-101 base keys, not active layout discovery. Packaging impact: none.

title: Fill remaining Kitty keyboard functional-key mappings
description: Audit yzt-7p3.27 found holes in the private-use functional/keypad table Rio can plausibly observe: KP_SEPARATOR 57416, KP_BEGIN 57427, MEDIA_REVERSE 57431 if distinguishable, and ISO level 3/5 shift 57453/57454 if exposed. Scope: map only keys actually represented by rio-window events, add unit tests, and document intentionally impossible mappings. Dependency decision: in-house/std only; this is table-driven encoding over existing key enums.
close_reason: Filled currently exposable Kitty keyboard table holes: KP_SEPARATOR for numpad comma, KP_BEGIN for numpad clear/begin, and ISO level 3 shift through AltGraph. Refactored mapping tables into pure helpers with focused unit tests, and documented that MEDIA_REVERSE and ISO level 5 remain unavailable because rio-window does not expose distinct events today. Verified with cargo fmt --check, nix develop -c cargo test -p rioterm --features 'rio-window/x11 rio-window/wayland rio-window/wayland-dlopen' kitty_ -- --nocapture, nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, and git diff --check. Only observed warning is the preexisting X11 IME function-cast warning.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Crate-vs-in-house decision before Rust work: use in-house/std only. This is a table-driven encoding update over rio-window Key/NamedKey/KeyLocation values. Production crates: none. Dev-only crates: none. Rejected alternatives: generated keycode table crate, because the Kitty table slice is small and spec-specific. Packaging impact: none.

title: Add black-box Kitty keyboard comparison fixtures
description: Audit yzt-7p3.27 calls for real key-sequence fixtures beyond byte-stream mode queries. Scope: capture/compare Rio, Ghostty, and Kitty for Helix/Yazi/Nushell/Zellij-oriented key combinations: Esc, Ctrl+C, Ctrl+Shift+I, Alt+[ , keypad arrows/operators, repeat/release, associated text, and report-all modifier events. Keep expected outputs source-linked to the official Kitty spec and black-box captures. Dependency decision: in-house/std plus existing Python harness only; no new crate needed.
close_reason: Added Kitty keyboard black-box case manifest, capture/verify harness commands, and documentation for Rio/Ghostty/Kitty comparison. Verified with python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, keyboard-list, synthetic keyboard-verify-capture, py_compile, and git diff --check.

title: Complete OSC 66 multicell editing semantics
description: Implement the full Kitty OSC 66 multicell interaction model after the first visual rendering slice: erase/replace whole multicell blocks on top-row overwrites, skip past lower-row block cells, erase intersecting blocks for ECH/EL/ED/ICH/DCH/IL/DL, and keep wrapping behavior aligned with the spec. Current implementation carries visual metadata through the side table and draws scaled/fractional text, but does not yet model every multicell editing rule.
notes: Dependency decision: use in-house/std only. OSC 66 editing semantics can be handled by scanning existing terminal grid text-sizing metadata and erasing intersecting visual blocks around existing edit primitives; no parsing or data-structure crate is justified.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 66 multicell editing semantics for top-row overwrite erasure, lower-row skip, width-fit wrapping, ECH/EL/ED erasure, ICH/DCH horizontal edit erasure, and IL/DL line edit erasure. Added focused backend tests and updated parity docs; split remaining cursor-shape expansion into yzt-7p3.38. Verified with cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, rio-backend text_sizing tests, cargo check -p rioterm, and cargo build -p rioterm.

title: Render OSC 66 multicell cursor shapes
description: Make block, bar, and beam cursors visually cover the full OSC 66 multicell character shape when the cursor is positioned inside a sized text block, matching Kitty text-sizing cursor semantics. The current OSC 66 work handles rendering and edit semantics, but cursor rendering still treats the cursor as a normal single grid cell.
acceptance_criteria: Cursor snapshot/rendering can discover the OSC 66 block under the cursor; block cursors cover the full block; bar/beam cursors cover the first column of the block; behavior is covered by a focused renderer/backend test or screenshot smoke.
notes: Dependency decision: use in-house/std only. The OSC 66 cursor-shape slice can reuse existing cell metadata and renderer cursor geometry paths; no crate is needed. Remaining risk is ownership across backend snapshot and frontend grid emission, not parsing or external data.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 66 cursor visual extents in renderer snapshots. Block and hollow cursor sprites use the full sized-text extent, bar/underline sprites use first-column width and full block height, block reverse-color uniforms mirror additional cells, and Ghostty shader cursor rectangles now use the sized-text extent. Added focused grid_emit tests and updated parity docs. Verified with cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, rioterm text_sizing_cursor tests, cargo check -p rioterm, and cargo build -p rioterm.

title: Add frame-time graphics benchmark harness
description: Add deeper performance instrumentation beyond the local yzt-7p3.15 smoke benchmark. Scope: first-frame latency, frame-time histograms during scrollback and shader animation, CPU/GPU utilization, memory, long-running shader stability, and graphics workloads for Kitty graphics/Sixel. Current benchmark docs intentionally stop at process startup/exit, PTY scroll-smoke timing, and screenshot evidence.
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision before implementation: use in-house Python/stdlib harness, no Rust crates. Production crates: none. Dev-only crates: none. Rejected crates/tools as hard dependencies: psutil, py-spy, perf, GPU vendor CLIs, because a portable first slice can use /proc, subprocess timing, and optional external tools only when present. In-house logic: frame-event log parsing, first-frame timing, frame-time histogram summarization, optional /proc CPU/RSS sampling, and CSV/JSON artifact writing. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Completed frame-time benchmark harness. Added env-gated Rio frame JSONL logging for RedrawRequested passes, a stdlib benchmark runner/summarizer with /proc CPU/RSS sampling and built-in scroll/idle/Kitty graphics/Sixel/shader workloads, and benchmark docs. Verification: nix develop -c cargo fmt --check; nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm; nix develop -c cargo build -p rioterm; python3 -m py_compile tools/yazelix_benchmark.py; python3 tools/yazelix_benchmark.py self-test; real frame-run smoke under nix develop recorded 3 redraws/2 presented frames; python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify; git diff --check.

title: Baseline build and launch upstream Rio
description: Build the unmodified Rio base, record toolchain and platform requirements, and capture either a launch screenshot or a clear build-failure note. This establishes the experimental baseline before feature work.
close_reason: Completed baseline build and launch report. Flake-shell cargo build succeeds; default native Vulkan launch fails in mixed Nix/host graphics environment; CPU renderer launch succeeds with screenshot evidence.

title: Create conformance and screenshot harness
description: Create a local harness for terminal protocol fixtures, visual screenshots, and version/source evidence. Initial scope: custom shader visibility, Kitty graphics, Kitty keyboard, OSC 8, OSC 52, OSC 66, OSC 133, synchronized output, and terminal identity.
close_reason: Completed conformance harness with byte fixtures, Ghostty cursor shader probe, environment capture, CPU-renderer screenshot path, docs, and verification evidence. Verified with python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, list, emit, record-env, launch-cpu-screenshot, and py_compile.

title: Prove Rio renderer can host Ghostty cursor shader parity
description: Spike Rio Sugarloaf/wgpu/librashader paths against Ghostty custom shader semantics. Decide whether cursor-aware uniforms, current/previous cursor state, palette/colors, focus/time, and animation hooks fit without an architectural rewrite. If not, document the pivot path.
close_reason: Completed renderer feasibility proof. WGPU build passes; source audit shows Rio already has cursor/palette/focus state plus a WGPU postprocess seam suitable for a dedicated GhosttyShaderBrush. Local GPU screenshots remain blocked by host/Nix graphics surface failures documented in docs/yazelix/dossiers/cursor_shader_parity.md.

title: Implement Ghostty-compatible cursor shader runtime
description: Implement the runtime/config path for Ghostty-style custom cursor shaders after the renderer feasibility decision is positive. Include shader loading, uniforms, timing, errors, tests, and a visual validation artifact.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: production crates remain on Rio's existing WGPU stack. Use existing wgpu 29 with the glsl feature enabled for Ghostty/Shadertoy fragment modules, existing bytemuck for uniform POD packing, existing tracing for visible shader load/render errors, and existing serde config plumbing. Do not add a new shader framework. Do not use Kitty implementation code. Build in-house Sugarloaf GhosttyShaderBrush beside FiltersBrush. Rejected alternatives: overload librashader filters because RetroArch presets do not expose Ghostty cursor uniforms; add a broad shader abstraction crate because WGPU already supports GLSL modules with naga; implement native Vulkan/Metal first because WGPU is the smallest existing postprocess seam. Packaging impact: WGPU builds already pay most dependencies, adding wgpu/glsl enables naga glsl-in for the WGPU feature path.
close_reason: Implemented first WGPU Ghostty-compatible cursor shader runtime: renderer custom-shader config, Sugarloaf GhosttyShaderBrush, Shadertoy prefix, std140 uniforms, Naga GLSL parse gate, active cursor/palette/colors/focus state, and render hook before existing filters. Verified cargo check -p rioterm --features wgpu, sugarloaf ghostty tests, rio-backend custom_shader config test, conformance verify, and dev build. Visual screenshot validation is blocked by local WGPU/native graphics launch failures, tracked separately in yzt-7p3.17.

title: Validate Yazelix Ghostty cursor shader presets
description: Run the existing Yazelix/Ghostty cursor shader presets in yazelix-terminal and capture visual evidence. Any incompatible preset gets a precise failure note or compatibility shim.
notes: Validation starts from the actual Yazelix Ghostty cursor shader presets in /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix. Use the WGPU/GL screenshot recipe from yzt-7p3.17, compile every preset through the Sugarloaf/Ghostty shader path, capture representative visual evidence, and document any incompatibilities precisely.
close_reason: Validated Yazelix Ghostty cursor presets against the WGPU shader path. Added Naga validation for generated preset shaders via YAZELIX_GHOSTTY_SHADER_DIR, moved the Shadertoy main wrapper after user shader source to support the real Yazelix generated shaders, extended the screenshot harness to load shader chains, and captured artifacts/shader_probe/screenshots/yazelix_default_cursor_stack_gl.png for the current dusk+sweep+rectangle_boom default stack. Verified with cargo fmt, sugarloaf ghostty shader tests against the generated Yazelix shader dir, rioterm cargo check --features wgpu, py_compile, conformance verify, git diff --check, and manual screenshot inspection.

title: Implement Yazelix mode without terminal-native workspace ownership
description: Add a Yazelix mode that runs one child command and lets Zellij own panes/tabs/workspace behavior. Hide or disable Rio tabs/splits/navigation in Yazelix mode unless explicitly running standalone.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new crates. Implement Yazelix mode in the existing clap CLI/config plumbing because Rio already has a single child command surface through -e/--command. Build the mode in-house by applying conservative config overrides: require a command, disable Rio split keybinding/config paths, default the app id to yazelix-terminal, and set TERM_PROGRAM accordingly. Rejected alternatives: adding a second launcher syntax, deleting Rio split code in the first slice, or introducing a Yazelix-specific config format before validating runtime behavior. Packaging impact: none beyond the rioterm binary CLI surface.
close_reason: Implemented the initial Yazelix mode CLI surface: --yazelix requires -e/--command, defaults app id to yazelix-terminal, sets TERM_PROGRAM to yazelix-terminal, disables Rio native split keybindings/config-editor split ownership, and documents the intended launch contract. Verified rioterm yazelix_mode tests, cargo fmt --check, conformance verify, git diff --check, and rioterm cargo check with platform features.

title: Extract Kitty file transfer session state from Crosswords
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision before Rust refactor: no new production or dev crates. This is pure ownership cleanup using existing std/base64 imports and current rio-backend types. Logic remains in-house because the code is terminal-protocol session state, path policy, and filesystem staging policy rather than a reusable third-party domain. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Extracted the OSC 5113 send/write state machine from crosswords/mod.rs into crosswords/kitty_file_transfer.rs. Crosswords now only routes Kitty file-transfer responses and approval requests as RioEvents. Verified cargo fmt --check, rio-backend osc5113 tests, cargo check -p rioterm, cargo build -p rioterm, conformance verify, and git diff --check.

title: Render OSC 21 visual-bell and transparent-background effects
description: Implement actual rendering behavior for OSC 21 visual_bell and transparent_background after the special color slots are stored/queryable. Visual bell likely needs a real Sugarloaf overlay/animation path tied to Bell events; transparent_background needs a compositor/window-background policy so it does not fight configured opacity, images, or per-panel OSC 11 backgrounds.
notes: Dependency decision: use in-house/std only. The visual-bell path is renderer timing plus an existing Sugarloaf rect primitive, and transparent-background composition is existing color state plus window-background policy. Production crates: none. Dev-only crates: none. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Rendered OSC 21 visual effects: BEL triggers a fading overlay colored by visual_bell, second_transparent_background/transparent_background drives transparent-window composition when no background image is active, Ghostty key alias is parsed, docs/conformance updated. Verified: cargo fmt --check; rioterm visual_bell test; rioterm transparent_background test; rio-backend second_transparent test; conformance verify; git diff --check; cargo check -p rioterm; cargo build -p rioterm.
